comments.text: Is this the same as the function $h'(n)$ defined such that, for any $m\geq 1$, in the interval $I=(m,m+h'(n))$ there exist $i$ multiples of $p_{\pi(n)+1-i}$ for $1\leq i\leq \pi(n)$?

With your function you would need $\frac{1}{2}\pi(n)+1 \approx \frac{n}{2 \log n}$ copies of $p_{\frac{1}{2}\pi(n)} \approx \frac{n}{2}$ which gives $h'(n) > \left(\frac{1}{4}-o(1)\right)\frac{n^2}{\log n}$. So I don't think it can be the same function.
